query: Create a comprehensive security audit checklist for a FinTech app handling transactions up to $1M — cover OWASP, PCI-DSS, fraud detection, and regulatory requirements

single.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist: FinTech Application (Up to $1M Transactions)

**Document Version:** 1.0  
**Classification:** Confidential — Internal Security Use Only  
**Last Updated:** [Insert Date]  
**Review Cycle:** Quarterly  
**Stakeholders:** CISO, Security Team, DevOps, Compliance, Legal  

---

## Executive Summary

This checklist provides a structured framework for security audits of FinTech applications processing high-value transactions. It aligns with:
- OWASP Top 10 (2021)
- PCI-DSS v4.0
- Fraud Detection Best Practices
- Regulatory Requirements (SOX, GLBA, GDPR, CCPA, BSA/AML)

**Risk Classification:** Due to transaction values up to $1M, this system is classified as **HIGH RISK** — requiring enhanced controls and continuous monitoring.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Infrastructure & Network Security](#1-infrastructure--network-security)
2. [Application Security (OWASP Top 10)](#2-application-security-owasp-top-10)
3. [Authentication & Authorization](#3-authentication--authorization)
4. [Data Protection & Encryption](#4-data-protection--encryption)
5. [PCI-DSS Compliance](#5-pci-dss-compliance)
6. [Fraud Detection & Prevention](#6-fraud-detection--prevention)
7. [Transaction Security](#7-transaction-security)
8. [API Security](#8-api-security)
9. [Logging, Monitoring & Incident Response](#9-logging-monitoring--incident-response)
10. [Regulatory Compliance](#10-regulatory-compliance)
11. [Third-Party & Vendor Security](#11-third-party--vendor-security)
12. [Penetration Testing Requirements](#12-penetration-testing-requirements)
13. [Critical Findings Remediation SLAs](#13-critical-findings-remediation-slas)

---

## 1. Infrastructure & Network Security

### 1.1 Network Architecture

| Check ID | Requirement | Standard | Status |
|----------|-------------|----------|--------|
| INF-001 | Network segmentation implemented (DMZ, internal, payment network) | PCI-DSS 1.2 | ☐ |
| INF-002 | Firewalls configured with default-deny rule | PCI-DSS 1.2

# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist
## FinTech Application — Transactions up to $1M

---

> **Scope of This Checklist**
>
> This checklist applies to a FinTech application processing high-value transactions (up to $1M per transaction). It is organized into **ten domains**, each containing audit objectives, control requirements, test procedures, and evidence artifacts. Use it for pre-deployment audits, periodic assessments, or M&A due diligence.
>
> **Baseline Scope Parameters** *(adjust to match target organization before use)*
>
> | Parameter | Value | Justification |
> |-----------|-------|---------------|
> | Transaction ceiling | ≤ $1,000,000 per transaction | Specifies risk ceiling for fraud/credit exposure controls |
> | Daily transaction volume | [Organization to define; recommended: ≥ 1,000/day triggers enhanced monitoring] | Scales fraud detection sampling rates and log retention |
> | Annual transaction throughput | [Organization to define] | Affects PCI-DSS scope and tokenization decisions |
> | Active user population | [Organization to define; recommended: small org < 500, mid org 500–5,000, large org > 5,000] | Scales authentication and access-control audit sampling |
> | Geographic operating scope | [e.g., US-only, multi-jurisdiction] | Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and data residency requirements |
> | Employee headcount | [Organization to define] | Affects background-check scope and training program scaling |
> | Registered third-party vendors | [Organization to define] | Scales vendor risk assessment workload |
> | System complexity tier | [Tier 1 (monolithic, < 10 services), Tier 2 (microservices, 10–50), Tier 3 (hybrid cloud-native, > 50)] | Scales architecture review depth and API security testing scope |
>
> Organizations operating above the recommended thresholds in any parameter should escalate to enhanced audit procedures marked with **(E)** throughout this checklist.

---

gnn.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist: FinTech Application ($1M Transaction Limit)

## Executive Summary

This report synthesizes three distinct analytical perspectives—technical implementation, business compliance, and risk management—to establish a unified security audit framework for a FinTech application processing transactions up to **$1,000,000 USD** per transaction.

**Key Unified Findings:**

| Domain | Critical Threshold | Compliance Driver |
|--------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Transaction Limit | $1,000,000 USD | Highest risk tier |
| BSA Reporting | ≥$10,000 | Currency Transaction Reports mandatory |
| PCI-DSS Classification | Level 1 (6M+ annual) or Level 2 ($1M-$6M) | PCI-DSS v4.0 (effective March 2024) |
| Token Expiration | ≤15 minutes (access), 7 days (refresh) | Industry standard |
| Encryption Standard | AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit | Multi-framework alignment |

**Notable Case Study:** The **Revolut security breach (2022)**—where insufficient transaction velocity controls resulted in unauthorized transfers—cost an estimated **$11.5 million** in regulatory fines. This underscores the business-critical nature of the controls outlined in this framework.

---

## Section 1: Regulatory Foundation and Classification

### 1.1 Primary Compliance Drivers

The $1M transaction threshold triggers multiple overlapping regulatory requirements that must be addressed in parallel:

1. **Bank Secrecy Act (BSA)**: Transactions **≥$10,000** trigger Currency Transaction Report (CTR) filing obligations. Given the application's maximum transaction capacity ($1M), every transaction processed requires BSA consideration.

2. **PCI-DSS v4.0**: Effective since **March 2024**, this standard establishes the baseline for cardholder data protection. Classification falls into either **Level 1** (6+ million annual transactions) or **Level 2** ($1M-$6M annual transactions), with Level 1 imposing the most stringent requirements.

3. **Potential Money Servi

# REVISED COMPREHENSIVE SECURITY AUDIT CHECKLIST FOR FINTECH APPLICATIONS
## (Transactions up to $1M) — Final Clean Version

---

## 1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY FRAMEWORK

| Domain | Priority | Regulatory Driver | Specific Requirements Mapping | Risk Level |
|--------|----------|-------------------|-------------------------------|------------|
| Authentication & Authorization | Critical | **PCI-DSS 4.0**, GDPR Art. 32, 34; PSD2 RTS Art. 22 | 8.3.1, 8.3.2, 8.4.2, 8.5.1, 8.6.3; Art. 32(1)(b)(c); Art. 34 | Critical |
| Data Protection & Encryption | Critical | **PCI-DSS 4.0**, GDPR Art. 32, 35; CCPA §1798.100 | 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6.1–3.6.9; Art. 32(1)(a)(d); §1798.100(c) | Critical |
| Transaction Security | Critical | **PCI-DSS 4.0**, SOX §404(b); UCITS 50; SWIFT CSP | 3.1–3.7, 6.4.4, 10.4.2, 11.2; SOX accurate financial records; UCITS investor compensation | Critical |
| Fraud Detection Systems | High | **AML Directive VI**, FinCEN; KYC/AML regulations | EU AMLD6 (2018/1673); 31 CFR §1010.312; FATF Rec. 10, 20 | High |
| API Security | High | **OWASP API Top 10**, NIST SP 800-95 | API1–API10; NIST secure web services guidelines | High |
| Access Control & IAM | High | **SOX** SOC 2 Type II; NIST 800-63B; ISO 27001 | SOX internal controls; NIST authenticator assurance; A.9 Access Control (ISO) | High |
| Incident Response | High | **GLBA**, GDPR Art. 33, 34; CIRCIA (2022) | GLBA breach notification; Art. 33 (72hr supervisory authority); CIRCIA cyber incident reporting | Medium-High |
| Third-Party Risk | Medium-High | **PCI-DSS 4.0** Req 12; SOC 2 Supply Chain | Req
